task: I’m sketching out a compact but very high-end PC build and want you to do the first pass of browser research so I have a clean shortlist to look at later. Start on Google and find the official ASUS ROG page for the ROG Swift OLED PG27UCDM monitor, because I want to anchor the build around the exact display model rather than a reseller listing; open the real ASUS product page and leave that tab open so I can glance at the specs and photos myself. Once that’s pinned down, use Amazon or Google search results to track down at least three reputable 2025 “best mini-ITX motherboard” recommendation articles from established tech sites, since I’m trying to match a small-form-factor motherboard to a premium monitor-and-workstation setup; open each recommendation page in its own tab so I can compare them side by side, and make sure you capture the page title, site name, and URL for each one. After that, go to B&H Photo and find the actual product page for the NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell graphics card as the GPU candidate for this same build, and leave that B&H page open too so I can verify it’s the real listing. In the end, send me the ASUS monitor page URL, the three motherboard recommendation entries with titles, sites, and links, and the B&H GPU page URL.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully navigated to the official ASUS ROG product page for the ROG Swift OLED PG27UCDM monitor (https://rog.asus.com/monitors/27-to-31-5-inches/rog-swift-oled-pg27ucdm/). The agent kept this tab open throughout the session, as confirmed by the final tab list in step 33. The URL is correct and points to the official ASUS product page.

R2: success - The agent successfully identified three reputable tech sites (XDA Developers, PC Guide, and WePC) with articles specifically focused on "best mini-ITX motherboards" for 2025. The agent opened each of these in its own tab and confirmed their presence in the final tab list (tabs 1, 2, and 3 in the final state).

R3: success - The agent successfully identified and opened three reputable 2025 mini-ITX motherboard recommendation articles. In the final response (Step 36), the agent provided the page title, site name, and URL for each of the three sources:
1. "Best Mini-ITX motherboards in 2025" (XDA, https://www.xda-developers.com/best-mini-itx-motherboards/)
2. "Best mini-ITX motherboard - high-end, budget and gaming - PC Guide" (PC Guide, https://www.pcguide.com/motherboard/guide/best-mini-itx/)
3. "Best Mini ITX Motherboard in 2025 | WePC" (WePC, https://www.wepc.com/reviews/mitx-motherboard/)

These details match the open tabs verified in the REPL logs (Step 33).

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to the B&H Photo product page for the NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell (URL: https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/1895402-REG/nvidia_900_5g144_2200_000_rtx_pro_6000_blackwell.html). The agent confirmed the page was open in the final tab list (Step 33) and provided the correct URL in the final response (Step 36). The verification confirms the URL is a valid B&H product listing for the specified GPU.

R5: success - The agent successfully gathered all the required information and presented it in the final response.
- ASUS monitor URL: Provided (https://rog.asus.com/monitors/27-to-31-5-inches/rog-swift-oled-pg27ucdm/).
- Three motherboard recommendation entries: Provided with titles, site names, and URLs (XDA, PC Guide, WePC).
- B&H GPU URL: Provided (https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/1895402-REG/nvidia_900_5g144_2200_000_rtx_pro_6000_blackwell.html).
All fields are present and accurate based on the navigation history.
